item.title: Version 2026.3.3
item.description:
What's New

Agent tabs stay interactive after the CLI exits

  • Exiting Claude or OpenCode inside a tab no longer leaves the tab frozen — you'll land back on a usable shell prompt, just like you would in a plain terminal
  • Slow shell startups (Oh-My-Zsh, powerlevel10k, custom dotfiles) are handled gracefully: the app now waits for your prompt to be ready before launching the agent, instead of using a fixed delay
  • A small "Starting …" badge appears briefly while the agent is being launched

Polish

  • The "Start New Session" modal is now labelled "New tab"

item.title: Version 2026.3.2
item.description:
What's New

Prompt files in the sidebar

  • Markdown and text files placed under docs/prompts/ in your workspace now appear in the Prompts sidebar, listed below your saved prompts
  • Double-click any file to copy its contents to the clipboard
  • Files are discovered recursively, so subfolders work too
  • Use the refresh button to re-scan after adding or removing files

item.title: Version 2026.3.1
item.description:
What's New

GhosttyKit update script

  • Added scripts/update-ghostty.sh to automate GhosttyKit.xcframework updates
  • Supports pinning to a specific Ghostty release tag

item.title: Version 2026.2.27
item.description:
What's New

Workspaces always visible

  • Workspaces panel is now pinned to the top of the sidebar, always visible
  • Explorer, Prompts, and Automation tabs switch independently below

item.title: Version 2026.2.26
item.description:
What's New

Terminal font scaling fix

  • Terminal font no longer appears at the wrong size when switching between displays with different DPI or when switching between tabs
  • Removed wrapper-side dedup cache that blocked necessary GhosttyKit reconfiguration signals
  • Hidden tabs no longer corrupt font rasterization state with invalid scale values
  • Tab activation now re-sends both content scale and size to GhosttyKit

item.title: Version 2026.2.25
item.description:
What's New

Display switching fix (follow-up)

  • Fixes remaining cases where terminal font appeared at the wrong size after switching displays with the same backing scale factor
  • Content scale is now sent unconditionally to GhosttyKit, matching Ghostty upstream behavior
  • Screen change handler now waits for the view's coordinate space to settle before reading scale factors

item.title: Version 2026.2.24
item.description:
What's New

Display switching fix

  • Terminal font no longer appears too big or too small when moving between displays with different resolutions
  • Closing the laptop lid or detaching an external monitor now correctly re-renders the terminal at the right scale

item.title: Version 2026.2.23
item.description:
What's New

Smarter keychain handling

  • No more surprise keychain permission prompts — when the OAuth token expires, the app shows a clear warning icon with a "Renew" button instead of silently retrying
  • Click Renew to re-authorize once, intentionally — the app explains what's happening and why
  • Keychain reads no longer freeze the UI while waiting for the macOS permission dialog

item.title: Version 2026.2.22
item.description:
What's New

  • Usage stats popover now opens on click instead of hover — no more accidental pop-ups
  • Added a close button to the usage popover for easy dismissal

item.title: Version 2026.2.21
item.description:
What's New

Usage stats in the status bar

  • Usage stats moved from the sidebar to the bottom status bar — compact colored bars show session utilization at a glance
  • Hover over the bars to see full usage details in a popover with provider tabs, metrics, and refresh
  • Sidebar is now cleaner with more space for the directory tree

No more keychain prompts

  • Fixed repeated "wants to access your keychain" dialogs — credentials are now cached after the first read
  • Stale tokens are automatically refreshed on authentication errors

item.title: Version 2026.2.20
item.description:
What's New

Bug fix

  • Fixed a bug where switching to a tab running a TUI app (opencode, codex) could cause text to appear very small due to an incorrect terminal grid size

Usage stats redesign

  • Provider switcher is now a vertical icon strip on the left side of the usage card — hover over each icon to see the provider name
  • Smoother animated transitions when switching between providers
  • Improved icon visibility in dark mode

item.title: Version 2026.2.19
item.description:
What's New

Sidebar

  • The directory tree no longer flickers when you switch working directories

Performance

  • Claude usage credentials are now cached, reducing repeated Keychain lookups when checking usage stats

item.title: Version 2026.2.18
item.description:
What's New

Usage stats

  • Usage data is now fetched once and shared across all tabs and workspaces — switching no longer triggers extra API calls or resets the timer
  • The "Updated" label now shows the exact time of the last refresh instead of a relative duration

item.title: Version 2026.2.17
item.description:
What's New

Claude tabs

  • Claude sessions now guard against rapid repeat submissions, helping prevent accidental duplicate prompts
  • When a submit is blocked, SlothyTerminal shows a short inline countdown so you know when to try again

Sidebar polish

  • The usage stats card is slightly taller, making session and weekly limits easier to read at a glance

item.title: Version 2026.2.16
item.description:
What's New — Usage Stats

See your Claude and Codex usage directly in the sidebar — no need to switch to a browser.

Features

  • Live usage dashboard — a new "Usage" section in the sidebar shows session and weekly rate limits with reset countdowns, always visible on every tab
  • Claude & Codex tabs — switch between providers with a segmented picker to see each account's stats at a glance
  • Claude: session & weekly limits — reads your Claude Code login automatically from Keychain, shows 5-hour session and 7-day weekly utilization, model-specific windows (Sonnet/Opus), and extra usage spend
  • Codex: session & weekly limits — reads your Codex CLI login from ~/.codex, shows plan, session and weekly utilization with reset countdowns, and credit balance
  • Usage settings — enable/disable stats, opt into experimental sources, import browser sessions, configure refresh interval

Security

  • Secrets stored in macOS Keychain only — never in config files
  • Browser/private source flows are opt-in and labeled as experimental

item.title: Version 2026.2.15
item.description:
What's New

Features

  • Reorder workspaces by dragging — rearrange your sidebar workspaces with drag and drop

Stability

  • Fixed terminal scroll jump when switching to a tab where Claude is working — the view no longer flickers to the top and back

item.title: Version 2026.2.14
item.description:
What's New

Performance

  • Smoother terminal rendering — duplicate resize and content-scale calls are now skipped, reducing unnecessary GPU work
  • Faster sidebar file browsing — directory scanning and icon loading now happen in the background
  • Faster Git overview loading — repository stats now load in parallel instead of sequentially
  • Reduced UI stutter when dragging or resizing the window — frame persistence no longer triggers full view refreshes
  • Revision graph is more responsive — stale loads from prior refreshes or rapid scrolling are automatically discarded
  • Status bar no longer re-fetches the git branch on every terminal command — only on tab or directory changes
  • Context menus in sidebar open faster — external app lookups are now cached at startup
  • Hidden tabs no longer participate in layout, reducing overhead with many open tabs

Architecture

  • Removed the native chat subsystem — all AI interaction now happens through terminal-mode tabs backed by OpenCode CLI
  • Simplified settings, sidebar, and startup flow by removing chat-specific UI
  • Hardened subprocess execution with timeout-aware async paths for Git and OpenCode CLI calls

item.title: Version 2026.2.13
item.description:
What's New

Stability

  • Fixed a bug where closing a workspace killed terminal sessions in other workspaces
  • Fixed stale split layout rendering when switching to an empty workspace

item.title: Version 2026.2.12
item.description:
What's New

Performance

  • Smoother chat scrolling during AI streaming — auto-scroll is now throttled and animation overhead removed
  • Improved terminal responsiveness — heavy output processing no longer blocks the UI
  • Chat session saves now happen in the background, keeping the interface snappy

Stability

  • Fixed a potential hang when exporting OpenCode session metadata (now times out after 5 seconds)
  • Reduced unnecessary UI refreshes when terminal busy state hasn't actually changed
  • Window title now updates correctly when switching between workspaces

item.title: Version 2026.2.11
item.description:
What's New

Make Commit Tab

  • Full Git staging and commit UI inside the Git client tab
  • Sidebar with staged/unstaged file tree, collapsible folders, and status badges
  • Side-by-side diff viewer with line numbers and color-coded changes
  • Commit composer with character count warning and amend mode
  • Double-click to stage/unstage files or entire folders
  • Context menu for discard changes and delete untracked files
  • New branch creation and push from the toolbar

Revision Graph

  • Selecting a commit now shows a side-by-side diff pane

UI Improvements

  • Close buttons on tabs and workspaces now highlight on hover
  • Fixed diff viewer width collapsing in both revision graph and make commit views

item.title: Version 2026.2.10
item.description:
What's New

Terminal Tab Command Labels

  • Plain terminal tabs now show the last submitted command in the tab title (e.g., "npm | cli" instead of "Terminal | cli")
  • Shell-aware parser handles quoted paths, environment variables, and wrapper commands like sudo and env
  • AI agent tabs (Claude, OpenCode) keep their static labels

Workspace Improvements

  • Empty workspaces now retarget to the new directory when creating a tab, instead of staying stale
  • Orphaned empty workspaces are automatically cleaned up when switching to an existing workspace for the same directory

item.title: Version 2026.2.9
item.description:
What's New

Git Client

  • Added a built-in Git client tab with repository overview and revision graph views
  • Browse repository stats and commit history directly inside SlothyTerminal

Startup and Workspace Tabs

  • Session creation now uses a reusable startup flow with richer working-directory handling
  • Workspace tabs now show numbered labels and support drag reordering
  • Added clearer drop indicators while dragging tabs

Fixes and Reliability

  • Fixed new workspace tabs incorrectly reusing the previous workspace folder
  • Fixed bottom bar branch display so it updates after branch changes such as git checkout
  • Cancelled tab drags now restore the original tab order instead of leaving partial reordering behind
  • Added a SwiftPM GitHub Actions workflow for Ghostty-free core validation

item.title: Version 2026.2.8
item.description:
What's New

Terminal Activity Tracking

  • Tabs now detect ongoing terminal output and show a busy indicator while AI agents are working
  • Activity auto-resets to idle after a short pause, keeping the tab bar responsive and accurate
  • AI agent tabs (Claude, OpenCode) are marked busy immediately on launch

Tab Bar Layout

  • Tabs now share available space equally instead of sizing to content, preventing overflow with many open tabs

App Icon and Build

  • New app icon
  • Separate debug icon so development and release builds are visually distinguishable in the Dock

item.title: Version 2026.2.7
item.description:
What's New

Workspaces and Sidebar Upgrades

  • Added Workspaces for grouping tabs by project
  • New sidebar panels for Workspaces, Prompts, Scripts, and Telegram
  • Project docs now appear in a dedicated section of the Working Directory sidebar

Terminal Automation and Activity

  • Added script discovery with quick path insertion into terminal tabs
  • Saved prompts can now be pasted directly from the sidebar
  • Inactive tabs now show a badge when new terminal output appears

Telegram and Internal Cleanup

  • Telegram bot now runs as a sidebar service with relay support for terminal tabs
  • Added a new terminal injection system for app-driven input actions
  • Removed the old Task Queue subsystem as part of the architecture cleanup

item.title: Version 2026.2.6
item.description:
What's New

Telegram Bot Integration

  • New Telegram Bot tab with passive/execute modes, event timeline, status counters, and activity log
  • Command support for /help, /show_mode, /report, /open_directory, and guided /new_task flow
  • Dedicated Telegram settings for token, allowed user, execution agent, auto-start, and open-directory defaults

New Startup Session Flow

  • Replaced legacy new-tab flow with a unified "Start New Session" page
  • Launch types now include terminal CLI, chat, and Telegram bot entries
  • OpenCode terminal launch supports predefined model and mode (Build/Plan)

Sidebar and Prompt UX

  • Added "Project docs" block in sidebar for README.md, AGENTS.md, and CLAUDE.md with quick open/edit actions
  • Prompts upgraded with reusable tags and richer prompt previews
  • Tab bar now shows animated execution indicator while work is running

Fixes and Reliability

  • Fixed Enter/Backspace macOS alert beep in terminal input handling
  • Improved terminal executing/idle state transitions via Ghostty command-finished callbacks
  • Hardened config loading and backward compatibility for removed native-agent keys

item.title: Version 2026.2.5
item.description:
What's New

Task Queue

  • Background AI task execution — queue prompts for headless runs without occupying a chat tab
  • Priority scheduling (High/Normal/Low), auto-retry with exponential backoff, 30-minute timeout
  • Risky tool detection (git push, rm -rf, .env writes, etc.) with post-completion approval gate
  • Live log streaming, per-task log artifacts, crash recovery for interrupted tasks
  • Task queue panel in sidebar with composer, detail modal, and approval banner

GPU-Accelerated Terminal (libghostty)

  • Terminal rendering now powered by libghostty with Metal acceleration
  • Full IME support for CJK and complex input methods
  • Reads Ghostty config files for terminal customization

OpenCode Ask Mode

  • New toggle instructs the agent to ask clarifying questions before implementing

Improvements

  • Smart Claude CLI path resolution — prefers native binary over Node.js wrapper
  • Chat input history navigation with up/down arrow keys
  • Context-aware streaming indicator showing active tool name
  • Suggestion chips on empty chat state for quick-start prompts
  • Configurable chat message text size, per-agent accent colors, terminal interaction mode

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed terminal prompt duplication on startup
  • Fixed IME candidate window positioning
  • Fixed crash recovery for interrupted task queue items

item.title: Version 2026.2.4
item.description:
What's New

  • Production chat architecture with session engine, transport layer, and persistent chat restore
  • New native OpenCode Chat with tool rendering, searchable grouped model picker, and Build/Plan mode controls
  • Improved stream reliability for Claude tool calls (multi-segment turn handling and parser fixes)
  • UI polish: compact native title bar, updated tab naming, and folder-based window title

item.title: Version 2026.2.3
item.description:
What's New

Chat UI (Beta)

  • Native SwiftUI chat interface for Claude CLI with streaming responses
  • Markdown rendering toggle, configurable send key (Enter / Shift+Enter)
  • Expandable tool use and tool result blocks
  • Chat sidebar with message count, session duration, and token usage
  • New menu item "New Claude Chat (Beta)" (Cmd+Shift+Option+T)

Saved Prompts

  • Create, edit, and delete reusable prompts in Settings
  • Attach prompts when opening AI agent tabs
  • Agent-specific prompt passing for Claude, OpenCode, and Terminal

Bug Fixes

  • PTY process cleanup on app quit — all child processes now terminated properly
  • PTY resource management overhaul — fixed zombie processes and leaked file descriptors
  • Fixed text selection in terminal (mouse reporting disabled)
  • Fixed opening files in external apps

item.title: Version 2026.2.2
item.description:
What's New

  • Minor fixes: Copypasting was worked incorrectly
  • Current Git branch is shown in the window bottom status bar

item.title: Version 2026.2.2
item.description:
What's New

  • Minor fixes

item.title: Version 2026.2.2
item.description:
What's New

Directory Tree Browser

  • Collapsible file tree in sidebar showing project structure
  • Shows hidden files (.github, .claude, .gitignore, etc.)
  • Double-click to copy relative path to clipboard
  • Right-click context menu: Copy Relative Path, Copy Filename, Copy Full Path

Open in External Apps

  • Quick-access dropdown to open directory in Finder, VS Code, Cursor, Xcode, and more

item.title: Version 2026.2.1
item.description:
What's New

  • Initial release
  • Terminal, Claude, and OpenCode tabs
  • Usage statistics sidebar
  • Automatic update support
